scope: Normative keywords in docs/standards/rfc/draft-hyprstream-resource-attestation-00.xml only. Each item maps one exact MUST/MUST NOT statement to an implemented test (exercised by the deterministic boundary checker and vectors owned by #1070) or a specification-only obligation with owner and blocker.

obligations.statement: A construction MUST NOT define a resource identifier as a hash or signature over another of these identifiers.
A finalized manifest MUST cite the stable resource identifier, the content CID it covers, its own manifest CID, and a deterministic operation ID independently.
obligations.test: python3 tools/check_resource_attestation.py (baseline manifest cites resource_id, content_cid, manifest_cid, and operation_id independently)

obligations.statement: Every request and external effect MUST be idempotent by deterministic operation ID.
A registrar MUST NOT finalize two successors for one operation ID.
A registrar MUST reject a manifest whose MAC attestation and ledger attestation are not over byte-identical canonical ResourceIntent digests.
A MAC-only or ledger-only attestation MUST NOT be accepted as a final resource manifest.
Provisional material produced by either attestation alone MUST remain unavailable through the normal namespace.
obligations.blocker: Namespace visibility gating by registrar state is not implemented; the durable registrar and mutable 9P ownership semantics are pending.

obligations.statement: Every transition MUST carry an expected predecessor and version, and the registrar MUST finalize it by compare-and-swap guarded by a fencing token.
Two concurrent transitions for one predecessor or version MUST NOT both finalize.
A stale, replayed, or crossed fencing token MUST NOT advance a resource version.
Every reservation MUST eventually post, void, or enter explicit quarantine or manual review.
obligations.blocker: The durable registrar saga, outbox, and reconciler liveness are not implemented.

obligations.statement: A crash or restart MUST NOT repeat a finalized transition or mint a second successor.
Provisional material MUST be unavailable through the normal namespace.
obligations.blocker: Registrar-state-driven namespace visibility gating is not implemented.

obligations.statement: Ordinary reads MUST remain MAC-only.
obligations.blocker: The MAC policy enforcement point is not wired to production read paths.

obligations.statement: Ledger and proof-plane I/O MUST NOT be placed on ordinary reads or on fine-grained 9P writes; bounded local leases settle at defined commit points only.
obligations.blocker: The read hot-path and fine-grained 9P write path do not yet enforce lease-only settlement.

obligations.statement: A classical outer admission result MUST NOT raise an inner resource operation to a post-quantum-hybrid assurance level.
Effective assurance MUST NOT exceed the assurance of any attestation it composes.
Anonymous authorization MUST NOT fabricate a DID or stable holder identity.
Identified, pairwise, and committed-owner profiles, identified and anonymous-capability controller profiles, and identified and anonymous-entitlement payer profiles MUST remain distinct.
obligations.test: python3 tools/check_resource_attestation.py (crossed-profile-kind and registry profile distinctness)

obligations.statement: A profile that accepts an anonymous controller or anonymous payer MUST NOT emit a stable holder DID, ATProto handle, or linkable entitlement identifier into an origin audit record, a public receipt, or a trust store.
Public proof records MUST use commitments; detailed receipts MUST be selectively disclosed or encrypted to authorized parties.
obligations.blocker: The selective-disclosure and encrypted-receipt proof-plane path is not implemented.

obligations.statement: A public receipt or checkpoint MUST NOT reveal a raw anonymous token, a holder DID, a stable client key, or a linkable entitlement identifier.
An encrypted receipt MUST be addressed only to a party authorized by the active profile and MUST be bound to the same canonical ResourceIntent digest as the manifest it accompanies.
obligations.blocker: The encrypted-receipt addressing and digest-binding implementation does not exist.

obligations.statement: Key-release and key-rotation history verification MUST NOT depend on a single classical signature over the resource history.
obligations.blocker: The key-history verification path and hybrid-PQC key rotation for resource history are not implemented.

obligations.statement: Unknown states, versions, profile kinds, or transition events MUST reject.
A CAS or blob integration MUST NOT expose provisional bytes as a finalized resource.
A content CID referenced by a manifest MUST match the bytes sealed by the content-addressed store.
obligations.blocker: The immutable blob write-then-seal reference integration does not exist.

obligations.statement: Mutable 9P title, directory-entry, rename, hard-link, bind, mount, and transfer semantics MUST follow only after the blob lifecycle and the crash and reconciliation suites pass.
obligations.blocker: Mutable 9P ownership semantics are not implemented and depend on the blob lifecycle and reconciliation suites.

obligations.statement: A 9P operation MUST NOT confer title that the registrar has not finalized.

A production registrar MUST NOT finalize a resource manifest until both reviewed attestations exist over the identical canonical ResourceIntent digest and the crash, concurrency, privacy, and interoperability suites pass.
obligations.blocker: The sibling implementation issues are not complete and the required independent reviews and suites have not passed.

obligations.statement: The experimental boundary checker MUST require a MAC attestation and a ledger attestation over byte-identical canonical ResourceIntent digests and MUST reject a missing, duplicated, crossed, or downgraded attestation.
obligations.test: python3 tools/check_resource_attestation.py (mac-only-attestation, ledger-only-attestation, duplicated-mac-attestation, crossed-resourceintent-digest, and downgraded-assurance)

obligations.statement: The experimental boundary checker MUST terminate a structurally valid fixture with the ConstructionIncomplete refusal, because the canonical production ResourceIntent encoding, registrar, and reviewed attestations do not yet exist.
obligations.test: python3 tools/check_resource_attestation.py (baseline structural success followed by ConstructionIncomplete refusal)

obligations.statement: Implementations MUST reject an unknown registry field or label, a non-canonical ResourceIntent encoding, or an out-of-bounds field before attestation or registrar processing.
